div1.entryFree:
Deadly, adv. 1) in a manner relating to or resembling death: how darkly and how d. dost thou speak, R3 I, 4, 174. d. pale, III, 7, 26.
2) mortally: d. hurt, Troil. V, 5, 12 (in Err. V, 70 it is adj.).
3) implacably: hate him d. Ado V, 1, 178. All's V, 3, 117. H6C I, 4, 84.
4) wickedly, detestably: they lie d. that tell you you have good faces, Cor. II, 1, 67.
